content:
In an 8085 microprocessor, the number of address lines required to access a 16 K bytes memory bank is

$\_\_\_\_$ .
explanation:
16 Kbytes memory bytes $=16 \times 1024$

$$ \begin{aligned} & =(2)^4 \times(2)^{10} \\ & =(2)^{14} \end{aligned} $$

The number of address lines required to access a 16 K bytes memory bank $=14 \quad$ Ans.

content:
The clock frequency of an 8085 microprocessor is 5 MHz. If the time required to execute an instruction is 1.4 $$\mu $$s, 
then the number of T-states needed for executing the instruction is

In an 8085 microprocessor, the contents of the accumulator and the carry flag are A7 (in hex) and 0, respectively. If the instruction RLC is executed, then the contents of the accumulator  (in hex) and the carry flag, respectively, will be
explanation:
Given that Carry flag = 0 and
Accumulator content = A7 H = 1010 0111

RLC means Rotate accumulator content 1 bit left without carry.

In RLC method every bit in accumulator move left side 1 bit and Most Significant Bit of Accumulator goes to the carry flag and in the Least Significant bit of Accumulator.

Here Most Significant Bit of Accumulator is = 1. So 1 will go to carry flag and in Least Significant bit of Accumulator.

After RLC method, in carry flag bit present = 1

And in Accumulator bits present = $$\underbrace {0100}_4\underbrace {1111}_F$$ = 4F H

Hence Option (D) is correct.

content:
The content of the registers are $R_1=25 \mathrm{H}, R_2=30 \mathrm{H}$ and $R_3 =40 \mathrm{H}$. The following machine instructions are executed.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \operatorname{PUSH}\left\{R_1\right\} \\ & \operatorname{PUSH}\left\{R_2\right\} \\ & \operatorname{PUSH}\left\{R_3\right\} \\ & \operatorname{POP}\left\{R_1\right\} \\ & \operatorname{POP}\left\{R_2\right\} \\ & \operatorname{POP}\left\{R_3\right\} \end{aligned} $$

After execution, the content of registers $R_1, R_2, R_3$ are
explanation:
For the mentioned register, we have $R_1=25 \mathrm{H}, R_2=30 \mathrm{H}, R_3=40 \mathrm{H}$, Now pushing $R_1, R_2$ and $R_3$

In First in Last out fashion or Last in First out Fashion, the values are popped out from the stack. So, the popped values

are $40 \mathrm{H}, 30 \mathrm{H}$ and 25 H consecutively and it gets stored into registers $R_1, R_2$ and $R_3$ correspondingly. So finally values of registers $R_1, R_2$ and $R_3$ are respectively $40 \mathrm{H}, 30 \mathrm{H}, 25 \mathrm{H}$.

content:
The following five instructions were executed on an 8085 microprocessor.
MVI A, 33H
MVI B, 78H
ADD B
CMA
ANI 32H

The Accumulator value immediately after the execution of the fifth instruction is

In an 8085 system, a PUSH operation requires more clock cycles than a POP operation. Which one of the following options
 is the correct reason for this?

options.content:
For POP, the data transceivers remain in the same direction as for instruction fetch (memory to
processor), whereas for PUSH their direction has to be reversed.
Memory write operations are slower than memory read operations in an 8085 based system.

The stack pointer needs to be pre-decremented before writing registers in a PUSH, whereas a
POP operation uses the address already in the stack pointer.
Order of registers has to be interchanged for a PUSH operation, whereas POP uses their natural order.

Both Carry flag and Zero flag will be reset

content: Write down the sequence of Instructions which are actually (till HLT instruction), if the program begins with the location 1FF5H.

The program given below is run on an 8085 based microcomputer system. Determine the contents of the registers: PC, SP, B, C, H, L after a half instruction is executed.
LOC
2000 START: LXI SP, 1000H
LXI H, 2F37 H
XRA A
MOV A, H
INX H
PUSH H
CZ 20 FF H JMP 3000 H
HLT
20FF ADD H
RZ
POP B
PUSH B
RNZ
HLT
answer:
PC = 3001 H
SP = OFFE H
BC = 200D H
HL = 2F38 H
